title: Keeping Quiet by Pablo Neruda
subject: English

notes.heading: Theme
notes.text: Need for introspection, peace, and universal brotherhood. Silence helps us understand ourselves and stop harming nature and each other.

notes.heading: Key Literary Devices
points: Symbolism: counting to twelve means time to reflect
Metaphor: Earth as a teacher showing life after stillness

summary:
In this poem, Pablo Neruda asks everyone to stop all activity for a moment and keep quiet. He suggests that we count to twelve and stay still. This moment of silence will be exotic because, for once, we will not rush, speak, or move.

During this stillness, fishermen would not harm whales, and men gathering salt would look at their hurt hands. Those who prepare for war with green wars, wars with gas, and wars with fire would stop. Soldiers in uniforms would walk around doing nothing. Neruda says he does not want total inactivity or death. He only wants a moment to understand ourselves.

If we were not so single-minded about keeping our lives moving, we could experience a sudden strangeness. This pause would give us time for self-introspection. We would realize that we are all together and that we hurt ourselves and others in our rush for progress.

He uses the image of the Earth to explain this. The Earth appears still but is always alive. Everything seems dead in winter, but later proves to be alive. Similarly, quietness is not death. When we keep quiet, we can understand ourselves better and create a world without war, greed, or destruction.

The poem ends with Neruda saying he will count to twelve and the reader should keep quiet too.

qa.q: What will counting up to twelve and keeping still help us achieve?
qa.a: Counting up to twelve and keeping still will help us stop all activities for a moment and think deeply. It will create peace, silence, and togetherness among human beings.

qa.q: Do you think the poet advocates total inactivity and death?
qa.a: No, the poet does not advocate total inactivity or death. He clearly says that life is important. He only wants people to pause for a while and introspect.

qa.q: What is the ‘sadness’ that the poet refers to in the poem?
qa.a: The sadness refers to human beings not understanding themselves and constantly threatening themselves with war, violence, and destruction.

qa.q: What symbol from Nature does the poet invoke to say that there can be life under apparent stillness?
qa.a: The poet uses the symbol of the Earth. The Earth appears still and dead at times, but later it proves to be alive by bringing new life.
